note: Baseline accuracy is 0% due to max_new_tokens=256 being too short for MATH problems. Paper uses 8000 tokens.
observation: All tested rock tokens (structural delimiters) show Neutral knockout effect, consistent with paper finding that 96.5% of rock tokens are Neutral
paper_key_findings: Pillars are rare: 3.5% on MATH-500, 1.5% on IFEval
Pillarhood is task-specific: no token is Pillar on both benchmarks
Strong Pillars are content-bearing tokens (certain, strategic, Initialize), not structural delimiters
Structural delimiters (which dominate rock tokens) are overwhelmingly Neutral
Our partial results confirm: structural tokens (the, $, **, space, We) are all Neutral
